more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: CNRG Petitions Parliament Over Environmental Destruction in Shurugwi
more.description: [263Chat] Civic organisation, the Centre for Natural Resource Governance (CNRG) has formally petitioned the Parliament of Zimbabwe calling for urgent intervention over alleged environmental destruction and human rights violations linked to mining activities in Boterekwa, Shurugwi.

more.title: Growth Ambitions Rise As CBZ Holdings Navigates Economic Headwinds
more.description: [263Chat] Zimbabwe's largest financial services group, CBZ Holdings, says it is on track to significantly expand its balance sheet and profitability despite mounting global and domestic economic pressures.

more.title: Waste Is an Opportunity' - First Lady Call for Shif to Green Economy
more.description: [263Chat] First Lady, Auxillia Mnangagwa has called for a nationwide shift in attitudes towards waste urging citizens to embrace sustainable practices that protect public health while creating jobs.

more.title: Zimbabwe Split as Public Weighs Electoral Reforms Bill
more.description: [DW] Zimbabwe has begun nationwide public hearings on a proposed bill that could extend President Mnangagwa's term and overhaul the electoral system. Supporters call it stabilizing, but critics warn it threatens democracy.

more.title: ZIDO sets stage for second edition of South Africa-Zimbabwe Business Expo in Johannesburg
more.description: The Zimbabweans in Diaspora Organisation (ZIDO) is gearing up for the South Africa–Zimbabwe Business Expo 2026, scheduled for May 22–23 in Johannesburg. The organisation is building on the success of its inaugural edition held last year, which attracted thousands of attendees and drew significant participation from both local and South Africa-based exhibitors.
